title: syzygium coarctatum
intro: Syzygium coarctatum, also known as the East Coast currant or bush apple, is a species of flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Australia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and fruits, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments. The leaves of Syzygium coarctatum have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and have also been used as a natural antiseptic. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and fruits of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, or used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
